frameworks.name: fronto-parietal attention network
frameworks.one_line: PPC carries prioritized attention signals and feeds back to sensory cortex.

frameworks.name: higher-order thalamic regulation of cortical functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Pulvinar gates information exchange between dorsal and ventral visual pathways.

frameworks.name: sustained delay-period activity in PPC
frameworks.one_line: Maintained spiking in LIP encodes attention without feedforward input.

core_question: Does the pulvinar regulate information transmission between LIP and V4 during sustained attention?
key_assumptions: LIP delay activity reflects attentional priority
Pulvinar is anatomically connected to both LIP and V4
Directed influence is measurable from simultaneous multi-area spikes
manipulations: Selective attention task with delay period
Simultaneous recording across pulvinar, LIP, and V4 in macaques
key_findings: LIP influenced V4 during the attention-task delay period
Pulvino-cortical effects were consistent with pulvinar sustaining LIP activity
interpretations: Pulvinar regulation of cortical functional connectivity generalizes to dorsal-ventral interactions
